name: Tennessee SNAP Benefits
description: Tennessee's SNAP program provides monthly food benefits on an EBT card through the Department of Human Services. Benefits help low-income individuals and families purchase groceries at authorized retailers.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 130% FPL; net income at or below 100% FPL

name: TennCare (Tennessee Medicaid)
description: Tennessee's Medicaid program providing health coverage to eligible low-income residents. Covers doctor visits, hospital care, prescriptions, mental health services, dental, and long-term care services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income children, pregnant individuals, seniors, and people with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Children up to 195% FPL; pregnant individuals up to 195% FPL; Tennessee did not expand Medicaid for adults

name: Tennessee Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary financial assistance to workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Benefits are based on previous earnings and last up to 26 weeks.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost their job through no fault of their own with sufficient earnings history
incomeLimitNotes: Weekly benefit based on highest quarter wages; maximum weekly benefit applies

name: Tennessee Housing Development Agency (THDA)
description: Provides affordable housing programs including homebuyer assistance, rental assistance, and homelessness prevention. Administers Section 8 vouchers, HOME funds, and the Hardest Hit Fund.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income homebuyers and renters in Tennessee
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally targets households below 80% of area median income

name: Legal Aid Society of Middle Tennessee and the Cumberlands
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income residents in 48 counties across middle Tennessee. Handles cases involving housing, family law, consumer protection, and public benefits.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals in middle Tennessee with civil legal issues
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of the federal poverty level

name: Memphis Area Legal Services
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income residents in the Memphis and western Tennessee area. Handles housing, family law, domestic violence, consumer, and benefits cases.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals in western Tennessee with civil legal issues
incomeLimitNotes: Generally at or below 200% of the federal poverty level

name: Tennessee American Job Centers
description: Statewide network of workforce development centers providing free employment services including job search, career counseling, resume assistance, training, and employer connections.
eligibilitySummary: All Tennessee job seekers and employers

name: Tennessee LIHEAP - Energy Assistance
description: The Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program helps low-income households pay heating and cooling bills. Administered through community action agencies across Tennessee.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households at or below 150% of the federal poverty level
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 150% FPL

name: Tennessee 211
description: Free, confidential service connecting Tennesseans to local resources for food, housing, health care, utilities, and other essential services. Available 24/7 by phone and online.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Tennessee seeking information about community resources
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services available

name: Second Harvest Food Bank of Middle Tennessee
description: A Feeding America member food bank serving 46 counties in middle and west Tennessee. Distributes food through a network of over 500 partner agencies including food pantries and meal programs.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in middle and west Tennessee

name: Mid-South Food Bank
description: A Feeding America partner food bank serving the Memphis and Mid-South area. Distributes food through partner agencies to individuals and families facing food insecurity.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in the Memphis/Mid-South area

name: Second Harvest Food Bank of East Tennessee
description: A Feeding America member food bank serving 18 counties in east Tennessee. Distributes food through over 500 partner agencies including food pantries, soup kitchens, and shelters.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in east Tennessee

name: Tennessee Department of Veterans Services
description: Provides services to Tennessee veterans and their families including benefits claims assistance, employment programs, and veterans' home care. Maintains offices across the state.
eligibilitySummary: Tennessee veterans and their family members

name: Tennessee Statewide Crisis Line
description: Provides 24/7 crisis support and referrals for anyone in Tennessee experiencing mental health crisis, substance abuse crisis, or emotional distress. Connects callers with local crisis services.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Tennessee experiencing a mental health or substance abuse crisis
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week

name: Tennessee Child Care Certificate Program
description: Provides child care assistance to eligible families to help pay for child care while parents work, attend school, or participate in job training. Administered through DHS.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with children under 13 who are working or in approved activities
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 55% of state median income

name: Tennessee Families First (TANF)
description: Provides temporary cash assistance and employment support services to low-income families with children. Includes job readiness activities and work participation requirements.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families with dependent children under 18
incomeLimitNotes: Very low income limits; varies by family size

name: Tennessee WIC Program
description: Provides nutritious foods, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and healthcare referrals for pregnant and postpartum women, infants, and children up to age 5.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant or postpartum women, infants, and children under 5 at nutritional risk
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of the federal poverty level

name: CoverKids (Tennessee CHIP)
description: Tennessee's Children's Health Insurance Program providing free or low-cost health coverage for uninsured children and pregnant women who are not eligible for TennCare.
eligibilitySummary: Uninsured children under 19 and pregnant women with income above TennCare limits
incomeLimitNotes: Children with family income up to 250% FPL; pregnant women up to 250% FPL

name: Nashville Metropolitan Transit Authority (WeGo)
description: Operates bus service in the Nashville metropolitan area with reduced fare programs for seniors, people with disabilities, and Medicare card holders. Access Ride provides paratransit service.
eligibilitySummary: Nashville residents; reduced fares for seniors, disabled, and Medicare holders
hours: Service varies by route; customer service Mon-Fri 6:30 AM - 6:00 PM

name: Nashville/Davidson County Metropolitan Action Commission
description: Community action agency providing comprehensive services in Nashville including Head Start, utility assistance, emergency food, weatherization, and workforce development programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in Nashville/Davidson County
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally at or below 200% FPL

name: Memphis Light, Gas and Water (MLGW) Plus-1 Program
description: Provides utility bill assistance to eligible Memphis residents through donations. Helps with electric, gas, and water bills for families facing hardship.
eligibilitySummary: Memphis residents facing financial hardship with utility bills

name: Tennessee Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides free energy efficiency improvements to low-income homes including insulation, air sealing, and heating/cooling system repairs to reduce energy costs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters at or below 200% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% of the federal poverty level

name: Tennessee Vocational Rehabilitation
description: Provides services to help people with disabilities prepare for, obtain, and maintain employment. Includes career counseling, training, job placement, and assistive technology.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals with physical or mental disabilities that create barriers to employment

name: Tennessee Promise
description: A scholarship and mentoring program providing two years of tuition-free community or technical college for Tennessee high school graduates. Covers tuition and fees not covered by other financial aid.
eligibilitySummary: Tennessee high school graduates attending eligible community or technical colleges
incomeLimitNotes: No income requirement; last-dollar scholarship after other aid is applied

name: Tennessee Reconnect
description: Provides tuition-free community or technical college for adults who do not already have an associate's or bachelor's degree. Covers tuition and fees at eligible institutions.
eligibilitySummary: Tennessee adults without an associate's or bachelor's degree

name: 988 Suicide and Crisis Lifeline - Tennessee
description: Provides free, confidential support for anyone in suicidal crisis or emotional distress. Call or text 988 to connect with a trained counselor 24/7.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing suicidal thoughts, mental health crisis, or emotional distress
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
languages: English, Spanish, and interpreter services in 150+ languages

name: Tennessee Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Provides crisis support, safety planning, and referrals to shelters and services for survivors of domestic violence across Tennessee.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing domestic violence, dating violence, or sexual assault
hours: 24 hours a day, 7 days a week

name: Knoxville-Knox County Community Action Committee (CAC)
description: Community action agency serving Knox County providing Head Start, energy assistance, emergency food, weatherization, senior services, and family development programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families in Knox County

name: Chattanooga Regional Homeless Coalition
description: Coordinates homeless services in the Chattanooga area including emergency shelter, rapid rehousing, permanent supportive housing, and outreach services for individuals and families experiencing homelessness.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals and families experiencing homelessness in the Chattanooga area

name: Tennessee ORCA (Office of Refugee and Immigrant Concerns)
description: Provides support services for refugees and immigrants in Tennessee including employment assistance, English language classes, cultural orientation, and social services coordination.
eligibilitySummary: Refugees and immigrants in Tennessee within their first years of resettlement
languages: English, Spanish, Arabic, and other languages
